---
title: "Stage-by-Stage Impact of Blind Hiring"
output:
  pdf_document:
    number_sections: true
header-includes:
  - \usepackage{booktabs}
  - \usepackage{threeparttable}
  - \usepackage{float}
  - \usepackage{bbm}
  - \usepackage{array}
  - \usepackage{arydshln}
geometry: margin=0.5in, landscape
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


<!-- +++++ ++++++ ++++++ Part 5-2: Analysis (Stage-by-Stage Impact of Blind Hiring) +++++ ++++++ ++++++ -->

\setcounter{section}{-1}

# Summary

\newpage

# Treatment Effect of Blinding on Hiring Outcomes (Stage-by-Stage Analysis)

## Blinding Disrupts Pre-Existing System of Demographic Premiums and Penalties

\begin{itemize}\setlength\itemsep{2pt}\setlength\parskip{0pt}\setlength\topsep{0pt}\setlength\partopsep{0pt}
  \item \textbf{Finding (i):} Blinding demographic information during candidate evaluation \emph{disrupts} a pre-existing system of \emph{demographic premiums and penalties}.
  \item \textbf{Finding (ii):} Blind hiring removes the \emph{baseline premium and penalty} that different demographic groups were experiencing at different phases of the hiring pipeline.
  \begin{itemize}\setlength\itemsep{2pt}\setlength\parskip{0pt}\setlength\topsep{0pt}\setlength\partopsep{0pt}
    \item \textbf{Winners: Hispanic Female in Resume-Screening (Phase 1) and White Male in Technical-Evaluation (Phase 2).} Blinding removes the baseline \emph{penalty} these groups received under non-blind evaluations. Their evaluation scores rise by \textcolor{blue}{+8.7\%} and \textcolor{blue}{+8.6\%} of a SD respectively.
    \item \textbf{Losers: Hispanic Male in Phase 1 and Hispanic Female in Phase 2.} Blinding removes the baseline \emph{premium} these groups received under non-blind evaluations. Their evaluation scores fall by \textcolor{red}{-11.5\%} and \textcolor{red}{-6.9\%} of a SD respectively.
  \end{itemize}
  \item \textbf{Caveat:} Hiring is often an \emph{ordinal ranking} problem --- one candidate's gain corresponds to another's lost opportunity --- so the absence of statistical significance on a given row does not mean the corresponding group is unaffected by blinding.
\end{itemize}
\vspace{-0.9em}

```{r blind_hiring_treatment_effects_demo, echo = FALSE, results = 'asis'}
## Treatment Effects of Blind Hiring (Overall (Z) only).
## Reference category: Blind resumes. Coefficients flipped post-fit so a
## positive coefficient = effect of blinding (relative to nonblind).

m_hr_blind <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
  feols(sc_overall_z ~
          i(race_gender, ref = "Blind") +
          resp_age
        | resume_index + r_do + resp_gender + resp_race + s2_educ + s2_income,
        data = firm_long_dt[role == "HR"], vcov = ~ responseid)
))

m_eng_blind <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
  feols(sc_overall_z ~
          i(race_gender, ref = "Blind") +
          resp_age
        | resume_index + r_do + resp_gender + resp_race + s2_educ + s2_income + resp_python + resp_java,
        data = firm_long_dt[role == "Eng"], vcov = ~ responseid)
))

flip_coef_signs <- function(model) {
  model$coefficients <- -1 * model$coefficients
  return(model)
}
## Use minimal column names; add_header_above sits above them with the
## panel labels and "Treatment Effects of Blinding for" header.
all_models <- list("HR" = flip_coef_signs(m_hr_blind),
                   "Engineer" = flip_coef_signs(m_eng_blind))

coef_map_blind <- c(
  "race_gender::White_Male"      = "White Male",
  "race_gender::White_Female"    = "White Female",
  "race_gender::Asian_Male"      = "Asian Male",
  "race_gender::Asian_Female"    = "Asian Female",
  "race_gender::Hispanic_Male"   = "Hispanic Male",
  "race_gender::Hispanic_Female" = "Hispanic Female"
)

gof_custom_blind <- tribble(
  ~raw,            ~clean,          ~fmt,
  "nobs",          "Observations",   0,
  "r.squared",     "R$^2$",          3,
  "adj.r.squared", "Adj. R$^2$",     3
)

## FE / controls footer rows. Respondent FE and Respondent Characteristics
## are placed adjacent to make the contrast visible: we do NOT soak up
## respondent heterogeneity with a fixed effect, but we DO include respondent
## demographic controls as absorbing FEs + age polynomial.
chk <- "\\checkmark"
nox <- "$\\times$"
fe_rows <- data.frame(
  term = c("Display Position FE",
           "Resume ID FE",
           "Resume - Coding Test Version FE",
           "Respondent ID FE",
           "Respondent Age (Cont) and Demographic FEs"),
  V1 = c(chk, chk, nox, nox, chk),
  V2 = c(chk, chk, nox, nox, chk),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
names(fe_rows) <- c("term", "HR", "Engineer")
## 6 coefs * (est+se) = 12 rows; FE rows (5) sit immediately after at 13:17.
attr(fe_rows, "position") <- 13:17

modelsummary(
  all_models,
  stars    = c('*' = 0.1, '**' = 0.05, '***' = 0.01),
  coef_map = coef_map_blind,
  gof_map  = gof_custom_blind,
  add_rows = fe_rows,
  escape   = FALSE,
  title    = "Effect of Blinding on Overall Evaluation Score by Race-Gender (Resume-Screening vs Technical-Evaluation Phase)",
  output   = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = "HOLD_position", font_size = 8) %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1,
                     "Panel A: Resume-Screening Phase" = 1,
                     "Panel B: Technical-Evaluation Phase" = 1)) %>%
  row_spec(12, extra_latex_after = "\\midrule") %>%
  row_spec(17, extra_latex_after = "\\midrule") %>%
  footnote(general = "Standard errors clustered by respondent.",
           general_title = "", threeparttable = TRUE,
           footnote_as_chunk = FALSE, escape = FALSE)

rm(m_hr_blind, m_eng_blind, all_models, coef_map_blind, gof_custom_blind,
   flip_coef_signs, chk, nox, fe_rows)
```




\clearpage
### Heterogeneity: Rejecting Traditional In-Group Favoritism
\enlargethispage{2.5in}

\begingroup\small
\begin{itemize}\setlength\itemsep{0pt}\setlength\parskip{0pt}\setlength\topsep{0pt}\setlength\partopsep{0pt}\setlength\itemindent{0pt}
  \item \textbf{Findings:} Our experiment demonstrates that engineers in the I.T. industry, when hiring for early-career software engineer roles, operate on a preference for the \emph{out-group} (i.e., a preference for \emph{under-represented groups}).
  \begin{itemize}\setlength\itemsep{0pt}\setlength\parskip{0pt}\setlength\topsep{0pt}\setlength\partopsep{0pt}
    \item When demographic information is blinded, the scores of \emph{concordant} (race/gender) applicants evaluated by Engineers rise by \textcolor{blue}{+6.9\%} of a SD ($p < 0.05$), while the scores of \emph{discordant} applicants fall by \textcolor{red}{-0.9\%} of a SD ($p < 0.05$).
    \item White Males make up the majority of the evaluator pool for engineers, and their behavior drives the aggregate results.
  \end{itemize}
  \item \textbf{Takeaway:} The majority gatekeepers were \emph{actively penalizing} candidates who looked like themselves and assigning a \emph{premium to evaluation scores of minority candidates} when such demographic information is visible. Enforcing blind hiring stops the majority group, White Males, from systematically boosting the minority candidates. This explains why, in our experiment, White Male applicants are the primary beneficiaries of blind hiring: they are ``rescued'' from the baseline penalty imposed by their own demographic engineer peers.
\end{itemize}
\endgroup
\vspace{-1.4em}

```{r bh_heterogeneity_concordance_combined, echo = FALSE, results = 'asis'}
## Combined panel: three concordance specifications x two roles, all on
## sc_overall_z. Race uses detailed Asian sub-ethnicity matching; Gender is
## broad Male/Female; Race AND Gender combines the two with detailed Asian
## sub-ethnicity. Respondents whose demographic is not represented in the
## applicant pool are coded as always-discordant in nonblind, so they stay
## in the regressions rather than being dropped.

firm_long_dt[, blind_concordance_race := fcase(
  treat == "blind",                                     "Blind",
  treat == "nonblind" & concordance_race_detailed == 1, "Nonblind_Concordant",
  treat == "nonblind" & concordance_race_detailed == 0, "Nonblind_Nonconcordant",
  default = NA_character_
)]
firm_long_dt[, blind_concordance_race := factor(blind_concordance_race,
  levels = c("Blind", "Nonblind_Concordant", "Nonblind_Nonconcordant"))]

firm_long_dt[, blind_concordance_gender := fcase(
  treat == "blind",                              "Blind",
  treat == "nonblind" & concordance_gender == 1, "Nonblind_Concordant",
  treat == "nonblind" & concordance_gender == 0, "Nonblind_Nonconcordant",
  default = NA_character_
)]
firm_long_dt[, blind_concordance_gender := factor(blind_concordance_gender,
  levels = c("Blind", "Nonblind_Concordant", "Nonblind_Nonconcordant"))]

fit_overall <- function(role_, conc_var) {
  ## Eng spec adds resp_python + resp_java FEs (Eng-only respondent traits);
  ## redundant under responseid FE but this spec doesn't have responseid FE.
  extra_fe <- if (role_ == "Eng") " + resp_python + resp_java" else ""
  fml <- as.formula(paste0(
    "sc_overall_z ~ i(", conc_var, ", ref = 'Blind') + ",
    "resp_age | ",
    "resume_index + r_do + resp_gender + resp_race + s2_educ + s2_income",
    extra_fe
  ))
  suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
    feols(fml, data = firm_long_dt[role == role_], vcov = ~ responseid)
  ))
}

m_hr_race  <- fit_overall("HR",  "blind_concordance_race")
m_hr_gen   <- fit_overall("HR",  "blind_concordance_gender")
m_hr_rg    <- fit_overall("HR",  "concordance_race_gender_detailed_blind")
m_eng_race <- fit_overall("Eng", "blind_concordance_race")
m_eng_gen  <- fit_overall("Eng", "blind_concordance_gender")
m_eng_rg   <- fit_overall("Eng", "concordance_race_gender_detailed_blind")

flip_coef_signs <- function(m) { m$coefficients <- -1 * m$coefficients; m }
## Order: left panel = HR (Resume-Screening Phase) with three concordance
## specs (Race, Gender, Race+Gender), right panel = Eng (Technical-Evaluation
## Phase) with same three specs in same order.
all_models <- lapply(list(
  m_hr_race,  m_hr_gen,  m_hr_rg,
  m_eng_race, m_eng_gen, m_eng_rg
), flip_coef_signs)
## Trailing-space disambiguators so modelsummary keeps each column distinct.
names(all_models) <- c("Race", "Gender", "Race+Gender",
                       "Race ", "Gender ", "Race+Gender ")

## Multiple raw names map to the same display label so the three
## specifications align on the same two coefficient rows.
coef_map_conc <- c(
  "blind_concordance_race::Nonblind_Concordant"               = "Concordant Applicant",
  "blind_concordance_gender::Nonblind_Concordant"             = "Concordant Applicant",
  "concordance_race_gender_detailed_blind::Concordant_Group"  = "Concordant Applicant",
  "blind_concordance_race::Nonblind_Nonconcordant"            = "Nonconcordant Applicant",
  "blind_concordance_gender::Nonblind_Nonconcordant"          = "Nonconcordant Applicant",
  "concordance_race_gender_detailed_blind::Discordant_Group"  = "Nonconcordant Applicant"
)

gof_custom_conc <- tribble(
  ~raw,            ~clean,          ~fmt,
  "nobs",          "Observations",   0,
  "r.squared",     "R$^2$",          3,
  "adj.r.squared", "Adj. R$^2$",     3
)

chk <- "\\checkmark"
nox <- "$\\times$"
fe_rows <- data.frame(
  term = c("Display Position FE",
           "Resume ID FE",
           "Resume - Coding Test Version FE",
           "Respondent ID FE",
           "Respondent Age (Cont) and Demographic FEs"),
  V1 = c(chk, chk, nox, nox, chk), V2 = c(chk, chk, nox, nox, chk),
  V3 = c(chk, chk, nox, nox, chk), V4 = c(chk, chk, nox, nox, chk),
  V5 = c(chk, chk, nox, nox, chk), V6 = c(chk, chk, nox, nox, chk),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
attr(fe_rows, "position") <- 5:9

modelsummary(
  all_models,
  stars = c('*' = 0.1, '**' = 0.05, '***' = 0.01),
  coef_map = coef_map_conc,
  gof_map  = gof_custom_conc,
  add_rows = fe_rows,
  escape   = FALSE,
  title    = "Effect of Blinding on Overall Evaluation Score by Evaluator-Candidate Concordance (Race / Gender / Race+Gender)",
  output   = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = "HOLD_position", font_size = 7) %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1,
                     "Panel A: Resume-Screening Phase" = 3,
                     "Panel B: Technical-Evaluation Phase" = 3)) %>%
  row_spec(4, extra_latex_after = "\\midrule") %>%
  row_spec(9, extra_latex_after = "\\midrule") %>%
  footnote(general = "Standard errors clustered by respondent.",
           general_title = "", threeparttable = TRUE,
           footnote_as_chunk = FALSE, escape = FALSE)

rm(m_hr_race, m_hr_gen, m_hr_rg, m_eng_race, m_eng_gen, m_eng_rg,
   all_models, coef_map_conc, gof_custom_conc, flip_coef_signs,
   fit_overall, chk, nox, fe_rows)
```
```{r het_pooled_master, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
## Master helpers shared by §7 / §8 / §9 (and their drop-age and
## drop-age + r_do_bythree subsections). The figure layout, labels, and
## extraction logic are identical across variants -- only the regression
## controls (age polynomial, position FE) and the coding-tier definition
## change.

build_aux_pooled <- function(d, coding_method = c("binary", "eqcount", "natural")) {
  coding_method <- match.arg(coding_method)
  d <- copy(d)
  d[, urm_tech_blind := fcase(
    treat == "blind",                    "Blind",
    treat == "nonblind" & urm_tech == 1, "Nonblind_URM",
    treat == "nonblind" & urm_tech == 0, "Nonblind_NonURM",
    default = NA_character_)]
  d[, urm_tech_blind := factor(urm_tech_blind,
    levels = c("Blind", "Nonblind_NonURM", "Nonblind_URM"))]
  d[, urm_axis_blind := fcase(
    treat == "blind",                                                            "Blind",
    treat == "nonblind" & race_gender %in% c("White_Male", "Asian_Male"),         "Nonblind_MaleNonHisp",
    treat == "nonblind" & race_gender %in% c("White_Female", "Asian_Female"),     "Nonblind_FemaleNonHisp",
    treat == "nonblind" & race_gender %in% c("Hispanic_Male", "Hispanic_Female"), "Nonblind_Hispanic",
    default = NA_character_)]
  d[, urm_axis_blind := factor(urm_axis_blind,
    levels = c("Blind", "Nonblind_MaleNonHisp", "Nonblind_FemaleNonHisp", "Nonblind_Hispanic"))]
  d[, race_gender := factor(race_gender,
    levels = c("Blind", "White_Male", "White_Female",
               "Asian_Male", "Asian_Female",
               "Hispanic_Male", "Hispanic_Female"))]
  if (coding_method == "binary") {
    d[, code_split := factor(top_half_coder, levels = c("0", "1"))]
  } else if (coding_method == "eqcount") {
    q_cuts <- quantile(d$test_case, probs = c(0.33, 0.67), na.rm = TRUE)
    d[, code_split := cut(test_case,
                          breaks = c(-Inf, q_cuts[1], q_cuts[2], Inf),
                          labels = c("Low_Coder", "Mid_Coder", "Top_Coder"),
                          include.lowest = TRUE)]
  } else {
    d[, code_split := cut(test_case,
                          breaks = c(-Inf, 5.75, 18.875, Inf),
                          labels = c("Low_Coder", "Mid_Coder", "Top_Coder"),
                          include.lowest = TRUE)]
  }
  d[, code_split := factor(code_split,
                           levels = if (coding_method == "binary")
                                       c("0", "1")
                                   else c("Low_Coder", "Mid_Coder", "Top_Coder"))]
  d
}

fit_pooled_v <- function(d, factor_col, split_col, age_terms, pos_fe) {
  fml <- as.formula(sprintf(
    "I(-sc_overall_z) ~ %s * %s + %s | resume_index + %s + resp_gender + resp_race + s2_educ + s2_income",
    factor_col, split_col, age_terms, pos_fe))
  suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
    feols(fml, data = d, vcov = ~ responseid + resume_index)
  ))
}

extract_pooled_te <- function(model, factor_col, split_col,
                              demo_levels_no_blind, demo_labels,
                              tier_levels, tier_labels) {
  cf <- coef(model);  V <- vcov(model)
  np <- length(cf);   cf_names <- names(cf)
  ref_tier <- tier_levels[1]
  rows <- vector("list", length(demo_levels_no_blind) * length(tier_levels))
  k <- 0L
  for (i in seq_along(demo_levels_no_blind)) {
    for (j in seq_along(tier_levels)) {
      d <- demo_levels_no_blind[i]; t <- tier_levels[j]
      v <- numeric(np)
      idx_main <- match(paste0(factor_col, d), cf_names)
      if (!is.na(idx_main)) v[idx_main] <- 1
      if (t != ref_tier) {
        idx_int <- match(paste0(factor_col, d, ":", split_col, t), cf_names)
        if (!is.na(idx_int)) v[idx_int] <- 1
      }
      est <- sum(v * cf); se <- sqrt(c(v %*% V %*% v))
      p <- 2 * pnorm(-abs(est / se))
      k <- k + 1L
      rows[[k]] <- data.frame(Term = demo_labels[i], Tier = tier_labels[j],
                              Estimate = est, `Std. Error` = se, `Pr(>|t|)` = p,
                              check.names = FALSE)
    }
  }
  out <- do.call(rbind, rows)
  out$stars <- ifelse(out$`Pr(>|t|)` < 0.01, "***",
                ifelse(out$`Pr(>|t|)` < 0.05, "**",
                ifelse(out$`Pr(>|t|)` < 0.10, "*", "")))
  out$Term <- factor(out$Term, levels = demo_labels)
  out$Tier <- factor(out$Tier, levels = tier_labels)
  out
}

make_pooled_plot <- function(plot_df, title_label, color_vals, shape_vals, show_y = TRUE) {
  ## Always include the y title on every sub-panel; patchwork's
  ## `axis_titles = "collect"` in run_section / run_section_cont
  ## consolidates the identical labels into a single one per page.
  p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Term, y = Estimate, color = Tier, shape = Tier)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black", linewidth = 0.4) +
    geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = Estimate - 1.96 * `Std. Error`,
                        ymax = Estimate + 1.96 * `Std. Error`),
                    position = position_dodge(width = 0.6), size = 0.3) +
    geom_text(aes(label = stars, y = Estimate + 1.96 * `Std. Error` + 0.015),
              position = position_dodge(width = 0.6), size = 2.6, vjust = 0,
              color = "black", show.legend = FALSE) +
    scale_color_manual(values = color_vals) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = shape_vals) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.3, 0.3)) +
    labs(title = title_label, x = NULL,
         y = "Effect of Blinding on Overall Evaluation Score (SD)",
         color = "Tier:", shape = "Tier:") +
    theme_bw(base_size = 8) +
    theme(axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, size = 7),
          axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8),
          legend.position = "top",
          legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 8),
          legend.text  = element_text(size = 7),
          plot.title   = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
          plot.margin  = margin(3, 6, 3, 3, "pt"))
  ## show_y arg retained for back-compat but no longer used: y title is
  ## kept on every panel and consolidated by patchwork at the page level.
  p
}

run_section <- function(coding_method, age_terms, pos_fe, file_suffix, header_label) {
  rg_levels <- c("White_Male", "White_Female", "Asian_Male", "Asian_Female",
                 "Hispanic_Male", "Hispanic_Female")
  rg_labels <- c("White Male", "White Female", "Asian Male", "Asian Female",
                 "Hispanic Male", "Hispanic Female")
  ut_levels <- c("Nonblind_NonURM", "Nonblind_URM")
  ut_labels <- c("Majority", "URM")
  ax_levels <- c("Nonblind_MaleNonHisp", "Nonblind_FemaleNonHisp", "Nonblind_Hispanic")
  ax_labels <- c("Male (NH)", "Female (NH)", "Hispanic")
  gpa_levels  <- c("Low_GPA", "Mid_GPA", "Top_GPA")
  gpa_labels  <- c("Low GPA", "Mid GPA", "Top GPA")
  gpa_colors  <- c("Low GPA" = "black", "Mid GPA" = "steelblue", "Top GPA" = "indianred1")
  gpa_shapes  <- c("Low GPA" = 16, "Mid GPA" = 15, "Top GPA" = 17)
  if (coding_method == "binary") {
    cod_levels <- c("0", "1")
    cod_labels <- c("Bottom 50% Coders", "Top 50% Coders")
    cod_colors <- c("Bottom 50% Coders" = "black", "Top 50% Coders" = "indianred1")
    cod_shapes <- c("Bottom 50% Coders" = 16, "Top 50% Coders" = 17)
  } else {
    cod_levels <- c("Low_Coder", "Mid_Coder", "Top_Coder")
    cod_labels <- c("Low Coder", "Mid Coder", "Top Coder")
    cod_colors <- c("Low Coder" = "black", "Mid Coder" = "steelblue", "Top Coder" = "indianred1")
    cod_shapes <- c("Low Coder" = 16, "Mid Coder" = 15, "Top Coder" = 17)
  }

  build_role_panel <- function(d, role_label, show_y_left = TRUE) {
    m_rg_gpa <- fit_pooled_v(d, "race_gender",     "gpa_tier3",  age_terms, pos_fe)
    m_rg_cod <- fit_pooled_v(d, "race_gender",     "code_split", age_terms, pos_fe)
    m_ut_gpa <- fit_pooled_v(d, "urm_tech_blind",  "gpa_tier3",  age_terms, pos_fe)
    m_ut_cod <- fit_pooled_v(d, "urm_tech_blind",  "code_split", age_terms, pos_fe)
    m_ax_gpa <- fit_pooled_v(d, "urm_axis_blind",  "gpa_tier3",  age_terms, pos_fe)
    m_ax_cod <- fit_pooled_v(d, "urm_axis_blind",  "code_split", age_terms, pos_fe)
    df_rg_gpa <- extract_pooled_te(m_rg_gpa, "race_gender",    "gpa_tier3",  rg_levels, rg_labels, gpa_levels, gpa_labels)
    df_rg_cod <- extract_pooled_te(m_rg_cod, "race_gender",    "code_split", rg_levels, rg_labels, cod_levels, cod_labels)
    df_ut_gpa <- extract_pooled_te(m_ut_gpa, "urm_tech_blind", "gpa_tier3",  ut_levels, ut_labels, gpa_levels, gpa_labels)
    df_ut_cod <- extract_pooled_te(m_ut_cod, "urm_tech_blind", "code_split", ut_levels, ut_labels, cod_levels, cod_labels)
    df_ax_gpa <- extract_pooled_te(m_ax_gpa, "urm_axis_blind", "gpa_tier3",  ax_levels, ax_labels, gpa_levels, gpa_labels)
    df_ax_cod <- extract_pooled_te(m_ax_cod, "urm_axis_blind", "code_split", ax_levels, ax_labels, cod_levels, cod_labels)
    list(
      rg_gpa = make_pooled_plot(df_rg_gpa, paste0(role_label, ": Per-Cell, by GPA"),    gpa_colors, gpa_shapes, show_y = show_y_left),
      rg_cod = make_pooled_plot(df_rg_cod, paste0(role_label, ": Per-Cell, by Coding"), cod_colors, cod_shapes, show_y = FALSE),
      ut_gpa = make_pooled_plot(df_ut_gpa, paste0(role_label, ": URM-tech, by GPA"),    gpa_colors, gpa_shapes, show_y = show_y_left),
      ut_cod = make_pooled_plot(df_ut_cod, paste0(role_label, ": URM-tech, by Coding"), cod_colors, cod_shapes, show_y = FALSE),
      ax_gpa = make_pooled_plot(df_ax_gpa, paste0(role_label, ": URM-axis, by GPA"),    gpa_colors, gpa_shapes, show_y = show_y_left),
      ax_cod = make_pooled_plot(df_ax_cod, paste0(role_label, ": URM-axis, by Coding"), cod_colors, cod_shapes, show_y = FALSE)
    )
  }

  dt_hr  <- build_aux_pooled(firm_long_dt[role == "HR"],  coding_method)
  dt_eng <- build_aux_pooled(firm_long_dt[role == "Eng"], coding_method)
  panels_hr  <- build_role_panel(dt_hr,  "HR",  show_y_left = TRUE)
  panels_eng <- build_role_panel(dt_eng, "Eng", show_y_left = TRUE)

  fig_dir <- here("2_Reports", "figures")
  if (!dir.exists(fig_dir)) dir.create(fig_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  plot_file_hr  <- file.path(fig_dir, paste0("8B_het_", file_suffix, "_HR.pdf"))
  plot_file_eng <- file.path(fig_dir, paste0("8B_het_", file_suffix, "_Eng.pdf"))

  fig_hr <- (panels_hr$rg_gpa + panels_hr$rg_cod) /
            (panels_hr$ax_gpa + panels_hr$ax_cod) /
            (panels_hr$ut_gpa + panels_hr$ut_cod) +
            plot_layout(guides = "collect", axis_titles = "collect") &
            theme(legend.position = "top")
  fig_eng <- (panels_eng$rg_gpa + panels_eng$rg_cod) /
             (panels_eng$ax_gpa + panels_eng$ax_cod) /
             (panels_eng$ut_gpa + panels_eng$ut_cod) +
             plot_layout(guides = "collect", axis_titles = "collect") &
             theme(legend.position = "top")

  suppressMessages(suppressWarnings({
    ggsave(plot_file_hr,  plot = fig_hr,  width = 10, height = 6.2, device = "pdf")
    ggsave(plot_file_eng, plot = fig_eng, width = 10, height = 6.2, device = "pdf")
  }))

  ## Escape underscores so r_do, r_do_bythree etc. render in text mode.
  header_label_tex <- gsub("_", "\\\\_", header_label)
  cat(sprintf("\\begin{center}\\textbf{HR Evaluators (%s)}\\par\\vspace{0.1em}\n", header_label_tex))
  cat("\\includegraphics[width=0.95\\textwidth]{", plot_file_hr, "}\\end{center}\n", sep = "")
  cat("\\clearpage\n")
  cat(sprintf("\\begin{center}\\textbf{Engineer Evaluators (%s)}\\par\\vspace{0.1em}\n", header_label_tex))
  cat("\\includegraphics[width=0.95\\textwidth]{", plot_file_eng, "}\\end{center}\n", sep = "")
  invisible(NULL)
}
```



```{r het_pooled_cont_master, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
## Master helper for the continuous-quality variants in §10. Replaces the
## categorical tier interaction with a continuous z-score slope:
##   race_gender * gpa_z      (and same for urm_axis_blind, urm_tech_blind)
##   race_gender * test_case_z
## Plotted TE points: -1 SD ("Low"), 0 ("Mid"), +1 SD ("Top") of the
## standardized quality score, so the visual layout matches §6-§9 plots.
## TE(D, q) = beta_D + q * beta_{D x quality_z}; SE via v' Sigma v.
## Resume-level main effects of gpa_z and test_case_z are absorbed by the
## resume_index FE; the demographic x quality interaction is identified
## from cross-resume variation in (race_gender, quality) pairs.

build_aux_cont <- function(d) {
  d <- copy(d)
  d[, urm_tech_blind := fcase(
    treat == "blind",                    "Blind",
    treat == "nonblind" & urm_tech == 1, "Nonblind_URM",
    treat == "nonblind" & urm_tech == 0, "Nonblind_NonURM",
    default = NA_character_)]
  d[, urm_tech_blind := factor(urm_tech_blind,
    levels = c("Blind", "Nonblind_NonURM", "Nonblind_URM"))]
  d[, urm_axis_blind := fcase(
    treat == "blind",                                                            "Blind",
    treat == "nonblind" & race_gender %in% c("White_Male", "Asian_Male"),         "Nonblind_MaleNonHisp",
    treat == "nonblind" & race_gender %in% c("White_Female", "Asian_Female"),     "Nonblind_FemaleNonHisp",
    treat == "nonblind" & race_gender %in% c("Hispanic_Male", "Hispanic_Female"), "Nonblind_Hispanic",
    default = NA_character_)]
  d[, urm_axis_blind := factor(urm_axis_blind,
    levels = c("Blind", "Nonblind_MaleNonHisp", "Nonblind_FemaleNonHisp", "Nonblind_Hispanic"))]
  d[, race_gender := factor(race_gender,
    levels = c("Blind", "White_Male", "White_Female",
               "Asian_Male", "Asian_Female",
               "Hispanic_Male", "Hispanic_Female"))]
  d[, gpa_z := as.numeric(scale(gpa))]
  d[, test_case_z := as.numeric(scale(test_case))]
  d
}

fit_pooled_cont <- function(d, factor_col, quality_col, age_terms, pos_fe) {
  fml <- as.formula(sprintf(
    "I(-sc_overall_z) ~ %s * %s + %s | resume_index + %s + resp_gender + resp_race + s2_educ + s2_income",
    factor_col, quality_col, age_terms, pos_fe))
  suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
    feols(fml, data = d, vcov = ~ responseid + resume_index)
  ))
}

extract_pooled_te_cont <- function(model, factor_col, quality_col,
                                    demo_levels_no_blind, demo_labels,
                                    eval_points, eval_labels) {
  cf <- coef(model);  V <- vcov(model)
  np <- length(cf);   cf_names <- names(cf)
  rows <- vector("list", length(demo_levels_no_blind) * length(eval_points))
  k <- 0L
  for (i in seq_along(demo_levels_no_blind)) {
    for (j in seq_along(eval_points)) {
      d <- demo_levels_no_blind[i]; q <- eval_points[j]
      v <- numeric(np)
      idx_main <- match(paste0(factor_col, d), cf_names)
      if (!is.na(idx_main)) v[idx_main] <- 1
      idx_int <- match(paste0(factor_col, d, ":", quality_col), cf_names)
      if (!is.na(idx_int)) v[idx_int] <- q
      est <- sum(v * cf); se <- sqrt(c(v %*% V %*% v))
      p <- 2 * pnorm(-abs(est / se))
      k <- k + 1L
      rows[[k]] <- data.frame(Term = demo_labels[i], Tier = eval_labels[j],
                              Estimate = est, `Std. Error` = se, `Pr(>|t|)` = p,
                              check.names = FALSE)
    }
  }
  out <- do.call(rbind, rows)
  out$stars <- ifelse(out$`Pr(>|t|)` < 0.01, "***",
                ifelse(out$`Pr(>|t|)` < 0.05, "**",
                ifelse(out$`Pr(>|t|)` < 0.10, "*", "")))
  out$Term <- factor(out$Term, levels = demo_labels)
  out$Tier <- factor(out$Tier, levels = eval_labels)
  out
}

run_section_cont <- function(age_terms, pos_fe, file_suffix, header_label) {
  rg_levels <- c("White_Male", "White_Female", "Asian_Male", "Asian_Female",
                 "Hispanic_Male", "Hispanic_Female")
  rg_labels <- c("White Male", "White Female", "Asian Male", "Asian Female",
                 "Hispanic Male", "Hispanic Female")
  ut_levels <- c("Nonblind_NonURM", "Nonblind_URM")
  ut_labels <- c("Majority", "URM")
  ax_levels <- c("Nonblind_MaleNonHisp", "Nonblind_FemaleNonHisp", "Nonblind_Hispanic")
  ax_labels <- c("Male (NH)", "Female (NH)", "Hispanic")

  z_pts    <- c(-1, 0, 1)
  z_labs   <- c("Low (-1 SD)", "Mid (0)", "Top (+1 SD)")
  z_colors <- c("Low (-1 SD)" = "black", "Mid (0)" = "steelblue", "Top (+1 SD)" = "indianred1")
  z_shapes <- c("Low (-1 SD)" = 16, "Mid (0)" = 15, "Top (+1 SD)" = 17)

  build_role_panel <- function(d, role_label, show_y_left = TRUE) {
    m_rg_gpa <- fit_pooled_cont(d, "race_gender",     "gpa_z",       age_terms, pos_fe)
    m_rg_cod <- fit_pooled_cont(d, "race_gender",     "test_case_z", age_terms, pos_fe)
    m_ut_gpa <- fit_pooled_cont(d, "urm_tech_blind",  "gpa_z",       age_terms, pos_fe)
    m_ut_cod <- fit_pooled_cont(d, "urm_tech_blind",  "test_case_z", age_terms, pos_fe)
    m_ax_gpa <- fit_pooled_cont(d, "urm_axis_blind",  "gpa_z",       age_terms, pos_fe)
    m_ax_cod <- fit_pooled_cont(d, "urm_axis_blind",  "test_case_z", age_terms, pos_fe)
    df_rg_gpa <- extract_pooled_te_cont(m_rg_gpa, "race_gender",    "gpa_z",       rg_levels, rg_labels, z_pts, z_labs)
    df_rg_cod <- extract_pooled_te_cont(m_rg_cod, "race_gender",    "test_case_z", rg_levels, rg_labels, z_pts, z_labs)
    df_ut_gpa <- extract_pooled_te_cont(m_ut_gpa, "urm_tech_blind", "gpa_z",       ut_levels, ut_labels, z_pts, z_labs)
    df_ut_cod <- extract_pooled_te_cont(m_ut_cod, "urm_tech_blind", "test_case_z", ut_levels, ut_labels, z_pts, z_labs)
    df_ax_gpa <- extract_pooled_te_cont(m_ax_gpa, "urm_axis_blind", "gpa_z",       ax_levels, ax_labels, z_pts, z_labs)
    df_ax_cod <- extract_pooled_te_cont(m_ax_cod, "urm_axis_blind", "test_case_z", ax_levels, ax_labels, z_pts, z_labs)
    ## Column titles only on row 1 (act as column headers); rows 2-3 use
    ## NULL titles. Y-axis title appears only on ax_gpa (middle-row, left
    ## column) and is stripped from all other panels so it is not repeated.
    title_gpa <- paste0(role_label, ": Treatment Heterogeneity by GPA")
    title_cod <- paste0(role_label, ": Treatment Heterogeneity by Coding Ability")
    list(
      rg_gpa = make_pooled_plot(df_rg_gpa, title_gpa, z_colors, z_shapes) + labs(y = NULL),
      rg_cod = make_pooled_plot(df_rg_cod, title_cod, z_colors, z_shapes) + labs(y = NULL),
      ax_gpa = make_pooled_plot(df_ax_gpa, NULL,      z_colors, z_shapes),
      ax_cod = make_pooled_plot(df_ax_cod, NULL,      z_colors, z_shapes) + labs(y = NULL),
      ut_gpa = make_pooled_plot(df_ut_gpa, NULL,      z_colors, z_shapes) + labs(y = NULL),
      ut_cod = make_pooled_plot(df_ut_cod, NULL,      z_colors, z_shapes) + labs(y = NULL)
    )
  }

  dt_hr  <- build_aux_cont(firm_long_dt[role == "HR"])
  dt_eng <- build_aux_cont(firm_long_dt[role == "Eng"])
  panels_hr  <- build_role_panel(dt_hr,  "HR",  show_y_left = TRUE)
  panels_eng <- build_role_panel(dt_eng, "Eng", show_y_left = TRUE)

  fig_dir <- here("2_Reports", "figures")
  if (!dir.exists(fig_dir)) dir.create(fig_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  plot_file_hr  <- file.path(fig_dir, paste0("8B_het_", file_suffix, "_HR.pdf"))
  plot_file_eng <- file.path(fig_dir, paste0("8B_het_", file_suffix, "_Eng.pdf"))

  ## Drop axis_titles="collect": y-axis title is now manually placed on
  ## ax_gpa only (see build_role_panel), so collection is unnecessary.
  ## plot.title.position="plot" + hjust=0.5 anchors the title to the full
  ## plot region (not the data panel), so titles in the left column are not
  ## pushed off-center by the y-axis text/title that occupies ax_gpa.
  fig_hr <- (panels_hr$rg_gpa + panels_hr$rg_cod) /
            (panels_hr$ax_gpa + panels_hr$ax_cod) +
            plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
            theme(legend.position    = "top",
                  plot.title.position = "plot",
                  plot.title          = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9, hjust = 0.5))
  fig_eng <- (panels_eng$rg_gpa + panels_eng$rg_cod) /
             (panels_eng$ax_gpa + panels_eng$ax_cod) +
             plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
             theme(legend.position    = "top",
                   plot.title.position = "plot",
                   plot.title          = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9, hjust = 0.5))

  ## Save shorter (height=4 instead of 6.2) so both HR and Eng fit on the
  ## same page as the §1.1.2 write-up.
  suppressMessages(suppressWarnings({
    ggsave(plot_file_hr,  plot = fig_hr,  width = 10, height = 4, device = "pdf")
    ggsave(plot_file_eng, plot = fig_eng, width = 10, height = 4, device = "pdf")
  }))

  ## Single-page layout: write-up | rule | HR | rule | Eng. No \clearpage.
  cat("\\noindent\\rule{\\textwidth}{0.4pt}\\par\\vspace{0.1em}\n")
  cat("\\noindent\\begin{minipage}{\\linewidth}\\centering\\small\\textbf{HR Evaluators -- Resume Screening Stage}\\par\\vspace{0.05em}\n")
  cat("\\includegraphics[width=\\textwidth]{", plot_file_hr, "}\\end{minipage}\n", sep = "")
  cat("\\par\\vspace{0.1em}\\noindent\\rule{\\textwidth}{0.4pt}\\par\\vspace{0.1em}\n")
  cat("\\noindent\\begin{minipage}{\\linewidth}\\centering\\small\\textbf{Engineer Evaluators -- Technical Evaluation}\\par\\vspace{0.05em}\n")
  cat("\\includegraphics[width=\\textwidth]{", plot_file_eng, "}\\end{minipage}\n", sep = "")
  invisible(NULL)
}
```
\clearpage
### Heterogeneity: Where the Demographic Premium Applies (Effects by Candidate Quality)

\begingroup\footnotesize\linespread{0.95}\selectfont
\begin{itemize}\setlength\itemsep{0pt}\setlength\parskip{0pt}\setlength\topsep{-2pt}\setlength\partopsep{0pt}\setlength\itemindent{0pt}\setlength\leftmargin{1em}
  \item \textbf{Findings:} The blinding effect on evaluation scores diverges across visible candidate quality --- GPA and Coding Ability (the success rate of the submitted code) --- within the same demographic group. The implicit out-group premium documented in §1.1.1 is concentrated at the \emph{top} of the ability distribution.
  \begin{itemize}\setlength\itemsep{0pt}\setlength\parskip{0pt}\setlength\topsep{-2pt}\setlength\partopsep{0pt}\setlength\leftmargin{1em}
    \item \textbf{Minorities.} In the \emph{resume-screening} phase, blinding removes the \textbf{premium} \textbf{Hispanic Females} previously received at all low, mid, high ability tiers, as defined by visible ability cues (\textbf{GPA} and \textbf{code success rate}). In the \emph{technical-evaluation} phase, blinding hurts \textbf{gender and racial minorities} by removing the \textbf{premium} they previously received under nonblind.
    \item \textbf{Majority (White Male mirror).} Blinding raises \textbf{White Male} evaluation scores roughly \emph{uniformly} across all ability tiers in both phases. The in-group discount evaluators apply to their own demographic under nonblind is a \textbf{flat demographic shift} --- not conditional on the candidate's observable ability. Blinding removes that shift, helping \textbf{White Males} equally at low, mid, and high ability tiers.
    \item \textbf{Takeaway.} Evaluators' demographic adjustments under nonblind are \emph{threshold-based}: \textbf{minorities} receive a \textbf{premium only when} they clear a high visible-ability bar --- concentrated at the \emph{top} of the GPA / code-success-rate distribution. Evaluators appear to want to advance minority candidates \textbf{but not at the cost of quality}, reserving the premium for the strongest-on-paper minorities and giving nothing to the rest. The mirror \textbf{penalty} applied to \textbf{White Males} is structurally different: it is \emph{unconditional}, a flat shift across ability tiers. Blinding removes both adjustments simultaneously.
  \end{itemize}
\end{itemize}
\endgroup
\vspace{-0.5em}

```{r het_pooled_102, echo = FALSE, results = 'asis', warning = FALSE, message = FALSE, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 4}
run_section_cont(age_terms    = "resp_age",
                 pos_fe       = "r_do",
                 file_suffix  = "102_cont_age1_rdo",
                 header_label = "Continuous quality, drop nonlinear age, r_do")
```


\clearpage
## Blinding Reshuffles Demographics of Hired (Composition Effect)

\begingroup\footnotesize\linespread{0.95}\selectfont
\begin{itemize}\setlength\itemsep{0pt}\setlength\parskip{0pt}\setlength\topsep{-2pt}\setlength\partopsep{0pt}\setlength\itemindent{0pt}\setlength\leftmargin{1em}
  \item \textbf{Findings:} Mirroring the per-resume rating effects in §1.1, blinding reshuffles who advances at each phase.
  \begin{itemize}\setlength\itemsep{0pt}\setlength\parskip{0pt}\setlength\topsep{-2pt}\setlength\partopsep{0pt}\setlength\leftmargin{1em}
    \item \emph{Resume-screening phase:} \textbf{Hispanic Females} gain a higher pass rate to the next round; \textbf{Hispanic Males} lose.
    \item \emph{Technical-evaluation phase:} \textbf{White Males} gain at the hiring stage; \textbf{Asian Females} and \textbf{Hispanic Females} lose.
  \end{itemize}
\end{itemize}
\endgroup
\vspace{-0.5em}

```{r blind_hiring_demographic_selection, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, fig.width=11, fig.height=7, out.width="0.95\\textwidth", fig.align="center"}

# ==============================================================================
# COMPUTE TREATMENT EFFECT AT EACH K
# LHS: hr_passk / eng_topk (0/1 selected)
# RHS: race_gender with "Blind" as reference, then flip signs
# ==============================================================================
results_all <- list()

demo_labels <- c("White_Male", "White_Female", "Asian_Male",
                 "Asian_Female", "Hispanic_Male", "Hispanic_Female")

for (k in 1:18) {

  hr_col  <- paste0("hr_pass", k)
  eng_col <- paste0("eng_top", k)

  # --- HR ---
  tryCatch({
    m_hr <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
      feols(as.formula(paste0(hr_col,
        " ~ i(race_gender, ref = 'Blind') + resp_age |
          resume_index + resp_gender + resp_race + s2_educ + s2_income")),
        data = firm_long_dt[role == "HR"],
        vcov = ~ responseid)
    ))
    ct <- coeftable(m_hr)
    for (dg in demo_labels) {
      coef_nm <- paste0("race_gender::", dg)
      if (coef_nm %in% rownames(ct)) {
        est   <- -ct[coef_nm, "Estimate"]      # flip sign
        se    <-  ct[coef_nm, "Std. Error"]
        p_val <-  ct[coef_nm, 4]
        results_all[[length(results_all) + 1]] <- data.table(
          demo  = gsub("_", " ", dg),
          Stage = "HR Stage", k = k,
          estimate = est,
          ci_low   = est - 1.96 * se,
          ci_high  = est + 1.96 * se,
          p_val    = p_val
        )
      }
    }
  }, error = function(e) {})

  # --- Engineer ---
  tryCatch({
    m_eng <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
      feols(as.formula(paste0(eng_col,
        " ~ i(race_gender, ref = 'Blind') + resp_age |
          resume_index + resp_gender + resp_race + s2_educ + s2_income +
          resp_python + resp_java")),
        data = firm_long_dt[role == "Eng"],
        vcov = ~ responseid)
    ))
    ct <- coeftable(m_eng)
    for (dg in demo_labels) {
      coef_nm <- paste0("race_gender::", dg)
      if (coef_nm %in% rownames(ct)) {
        est   <- -ct[coef_nm, "Estimate"]      # flip sign
        se    <-  ct[coef_nm, "Std. Error"]
        p_val <-  ct[coef_nm, 4]
        results_all[[length(results_all) + 1]] <- data.table(
          demo  = gsub("_", " ", dg),
          Stage = "Engineer Stage", k = k,
          estimate = est,
          ci_low   = est - 1.96 * se,
          ci_high  = est + 1.96 * se,
          p_val    = p_val
        )
      }
    }
  }, error = function(e) {})
}

# ==============================================================================
# ASSEMBLE AND PLOT
# ==============================================================================
dt_plot <- rbindlist(results_all)
dt_plot[, Stage := factor(Stage, levels = c("HR Stage", "Engineer Stage"))]
dt_plot[, demo  := factor(demo,  levels = c("White Male",    "White Female",
                                             "Asian Male",    "Asian Female",
                                             "Hispanic Male", "Hispanic Female"))]
dt_plot[, sig_stars := fcase(
  p_val < 0.01, "***",
  p_val < 0.05, "**",
  p_val < 0.10, "*",
  default = ""
)]
dt_plot[, star_y := ifelse(estimate >= 0,
                            ci_high + 0.005,
                            ci_low  - 0.005)]

ggplot(dt_plot, aes(x = k, y = estimate, color = Stage)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = ci_low, ymax = ci_high),
                  linewidth = 0.6, size = 0.4, fatten = 3) +
  geom_text(aes(y = star_y, label = sig_stars),
            vjust = ifelse(dt_plot$estimate >= 0, 0, 1),
            size = 3, color = "black", show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_grid(demo ~ Stage) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("HR Stage" = "#C44E52", "Engineer Stage" = "#4C72B0")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:18,
                     name   = "Number of Top Candidates Selected (K)") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.20))) +
  labs(
    title    = "Effect of Blind Hiring on Selection Probability by Demographic Group",
    subtitle = paste("Blinding effect = -(effect of revealing identity vs blind).",
                     "Positive = more likely selected under blinding."),
    y        = "Blinding Effect on Selection Probability",
    color    = "Evaluator Role",
    caption  = "Significance: * p<0.10, ** p<0.05, *** p<0.01. Base group: Blind resumes."
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    legend.text        = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray30"),
    strip.text         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    strip.text.y       = element_text(angle = 0),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(margin = margin(t = 10)),
    axis.title.y       = element_text(margin = margin(r = 10)),
    plot.caption       = element_text(size = 8, hjust = 0),
    panel.spacing      = unit(0.4, "lines")
  )

rm(results_all, dt_plot, demo_labels, dg, k,
   hr_col, eng_col, m_hr, m_eng, ct,
   est, se, p_val, coef_nm)
```


\clearpage
# Effect of Blinding on Evaluation Behavior


## Time Spent Per Resume and Material Engagement

\begingroup\footnotesize\linespread{0.95}\selectfont
\begin{itemize}\setlength\itemsep{0pt}\setlength\parskip{0pt}\setlength\topsep{-2pt}\setlength\partopsep{0pt}\setlength\itemindent{0pt}\setlength\leftmargin{1em}
  \item \textbf{Findings:} Blinding does not change the \textbf{time} evaluators spend per resume in either phase. It does, however, change \textbf{what they engage with}: \textbf{Engineer evaluators} open candidates' resume PDF and coding scripts at a $\sim$10 pp higher rate under blinding than under nonblind.
\end{itemize}
\endgroup
\vspace{-0.5em}

```{r blind_2p1_sidebyside, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
## Derived vars (used by both tables).
firm_long_dt[, ok_ton          := !is.na(timeonpage) & timeonpage <= 600]
firm_long_dt[, blind           := as.integer(treat == "blind")]
firm_long_dt[, test_case_z     := as.numeric(scale(test_case))]
firm_long_dt[, open_code1_or_2 := as.integer(
  (!is.na(open_code1) & open_code1 == 1) | (!is.na(open_code2) & open_code2 == 1))]

build_kbl <- function(models, coef_map, fe_rows, header_panels, font, footnote_text) {
  modelsummary(
    models,
    stars    = c('*' = 0.1, '**' = 0.05, '***' = 0.01),
    coef_map = coef_map,
    gof_map  = tribble(~raw, ~clean, ~fmt,
                        "nobs",          "Observations", 0,
                        "r.squared",     "R$^2$",        3,
                        "adj.r.squared", "Adj. R$^2$",   3),
    add_rows = fe_rows,
    escape   = FALSE,
    output   = "kableExtra"
  ) %>%
    kable_styling(latex_options = "HOLD_position", font_size = font) %>%
    add_header_above(header_panels) %>%
    row_spec(1, extra_latex_after = "\\hdashline") %>%
    row_spec(5, extra_latex_after = "\\midrule") %>%
    row_spec(10, extra_latex_after = "\\midrule") %>%
    footnote(general = footnote_text, general_title = "",
             threeparttable = TRUE, footnote_as_chunk = FALSE, escape = FALSE)
}

strip_table_env <- function(kbl) {
  s <- paste(as.character(kbl), collapse = "\n")
  s <- gsub("\\\\begin\\{table\\}[^\n]*\n?", "", s)
  s <- gsub("\\\\end\\{table\\}\\s*", "", s)
  s
}

## ---------------- Time spent (HR + Eng, 4 cols) -----------------
fit_t <- function(role_lab, het = FALSE) {
  d <- firm_long_dt[role == role_lab & ok_ton]
  if (het) d <- d[!is.na(test_case_z)]
  if (het) { d <- copy(d); d[, q_int := blind * test_case_z] }
  fe_extra <- if (role_lab == "Eng") " + resp_python + resp_java" else ""
  rhs <- if (het) "blind + q_int + resp_age" else "blind + resp_age"
  feols(as.formula(sprintf("timeonpage ~ %s | resume_index + r_do + resp_gender + resp_race + s2_educ + s2_income%s",
                            rhs, fe_extra)),
        data = d, vcov = ~ responseid)
}
m_t_hr1  <- fit_t("HR",  FALSE);  m_t_hr2  <- fit_t("HR",  TRUE)
m_t_eng1 <- fit_t("Eng", FALSE);  m_t_eng2 <- fit_t("Eng", TRUE)
nb_mean_hr  <- mean(firm_long_dt[role == "HR"  & treat == "nonblind" & ok_ton, timeonpage], na.rm = TRUE)
nb_mean_eng <- mean(firm_long_dt[role == "Eng" & treat == "nonblind" & ok_ton, timeonpage], na.rm = TRUE)

chk <- "\\checkmark"; nox <- "$\\times$"
chk_mc <- "\\makecell[c]{\\checkmark}"; nox_mc <- "\\makecell[c]{$\\times$}"
fe_rows_t <- data.frame(
  term = c("Nonblind Group Mean (Seconds)",
           "Display Position FE",
           "Resume ID FE",
           "Resume - Coding Test Version FE",
           "Respondent ID FE",
           "\\makecell[l]{Respondent Age (Cont)\\\\\\quad and Demographic FEs}"),
  V1 = c(sprintf("%.1f", nb_mean_hr),  chk, chk, nox, nox, chk_mc),
  V2 = c(sprintf("%.1f", nb_mean_hr),  chk, chk, nox, nox, chk_mc),
  V3 = c(sprintf("%.1f", nb_mean_eng), chk, chk, nox, nox, chk_mc),
  V4 = c(sprintf("%.1f", nb_mean_eng), chk, chk, nox, nox, chk_mc),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
names(fe_rows_t) <- c("term", "(1)", "(2)", "(3)", "(4)")
attr(fe_rows_t, "position") <- c(1, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10)

time_kbl <- build_kbl(
  list("(1)" = m_t_hr1, "(2)" = m_t_hr2, "(3)" = m_t_eng1, "(4)" = m_t_eng2),
  c("blind" = "Blind Treatment",
    "q_int" = "Blind $\\times$ Coding Ability (Z)"),
  fe_rows_t,
  c(" " = 1, "Panel A: Resume-Screening Phase" = 2, "Panel B: Technical-Evaluation Phase" = 2),
  font = 7,
  footnote_text = "Outcome in seconds. Standard errors clustered by respondent."
)
time_str <- strip_table_env(time_kbl)

## ---------------- Open rates (Eng only, 4 cols) -----------------
eng <- firm_long_dt[role == "Eng"]
fit_o <- function(dv, het = FALSE) {
  d <- eng
  if (het) d <- d[!is.na(test_case_z)]
  if (het) { d <- copy(d); d[, q_int := blind * test_case_z] }
  rhs <- if (het) "blind + q_int + resp_age" else "blind + resp_age"
  feols(as.formula(sprintf("%s ~ %s | resume_index + r_do + resp_gender + resp_race + s2_educ + s2_income + resp_python + resp_java",
                            dv, rhs)),
        data = d, vcov = ~ responseid)
}
m_or1  <- fit_o("open_resume",     FALSE); m_or2  <- fit_o("open_resume",     TRUE)
m_oc1  <- fit_o("open_code1_or_2", FALSE); m_oc2  <- fit_o("open_code1_or_2", TRUE)
nb_mean_or <- mean(eng[treat == "nonblind", open_resume],     na.rm = TRUE)
nb_mean_oc <- mean(eng[treat == "nonblind", open_code1_or_2], na.rm = TRUE)

fe_rows_o <- data.frame(
  term = c("Nonblind Group Mean (Open Rate)",
           "Display Position FE",
           "Resume ID FE",
           "Resume - Coding Test Version FE",
           "Respondent ID FE",
           "\\makecell[l]{Respondent Age (Cont)\\\\\\quad and Demographic FEs}"),
  V1 = c(sprintf("%.3f", nb_mean_or), chk, chk, nox, nox, chk_mc),
  V2 = c(sprintf("%.3f", nb_mean_or), chk, chk, nox, nox, chk_mc),
  V3 = c(sprintf("%.3f", nb_mean_oc), chk, chk, nox, nox, chk_mc),
  V4 = c(sprintf("%.3f", nb_mean_oc), chk, chk, nox, nox, chk_mc),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
names(fe_rows_o) <- c("term", "(1)", "(2)", "(3)", "(4)")
attr(fe_rows_o, "position") <- c(1, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10)

open_kbl <- build_kbl(
  list("(1)" = m_or1, "(2)" = m_or2, "(3)" = m_oc1, "(4)" = m_oc2),
  c("blind" = "Blind Treatment",
    "q_int" = "Blind $\\times$ Coding Ability (Z)"),
  fe_rows_o,
  c(" " = 1, "Panel A: Open Resume" = 2, "Panel B: Open Codes (1/2)" = 2),
  font = 6,
  footnote_text = "Outcome is 0/1 (file opened). Standard errors clustered by respondent."
)
open_str <- strip_table_env(open_kbl)

## ---------------- Side-by-side layout (60% / 40%) ---------------
cat("```{=latex}\n")
cat("\\noindent\\begin{minipage}[t]{0.60\\textwidth}\\centering\n")
cat("\\refstepcounter{table}\\textbf{Table~\\thetable: Effect of Blinding on Time Spent Per Resume}\\par\\smallskip\n")
cat(time_str, "\n", sep = "")
cat("\\end{minipage}\\hfill\n")
cat("\\begin{minipage}[t]{0.39\\textwidth}\\centering\n")
cat("\\refstepcounter{table}\\textbf{Table~\\thetable: Material Engagement (Engineer Only)}\\par\\smallskip\n")
cat(open_str, "\n", sep = "")
cat("\\end{minipage}\n")
cat("```\n")

rm(m_t_hr1, m_t_hr2, m_t_eng1, m_t_eng2, m_or1, m_or2, m_oc1, m_oc2,
   nb_mean_hr, nb_mean_eng, nb_mean_or, nb_mean_oc,
   fe_rows_t, fe_rows_o, time_kbl, open_kbl, time_str, open_str,
   build_kbl, strip_table_env, fit_t, fit_o, eng, chk, nox)
```


\clearpage
## Signal Substitution -- Re-weighting of Visible Resume Cues

\textbf{Question:} What visible resume signals does the evaluator lean on more (or less) under blinding?

\textbf{Specification:}
\vspace{-0.7em}
$$
\begin{aligned}
\text{Overall\_Evaluation\_Score~(Z)}_{ij} \;\sim\;& \sum_{s \in \mathcal{S}_{\text{role}}} \boldsymbol{\beta_s}\,\text{Signal}_s^i \times \mathbbm{1}[\text{Blind}]_j \;\Big|\; \text{Resume\_ID~FE}_i + \text{Respondent\_ID~FE}_j + \text{Display\_Order~FE}_{ij} \\[2pt]
& \text{Two-way clustered SEs on (responseid, resume\_id).}
\end{aligned}
$$
\vspace{-1em}
\enlargethispage{2.5in}

```{r signal_substitution_pooled, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

if (!"resume_id" %in% names(firm_long_dt)) {
  firm_long_dt[, resume_id := paste(resume_ver, resume_index, sep = "_")]
}

## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
## Visible-signal sets, by evaluator role.
##  Removed for collinearity / no within-sample variation:
##    - `python`, `java`: listed by every one of the 18 candidates
##      (constants), so absorbed by resume_index FE -- no identification.
##    - `q1_cplusplus`, `q2_cplusplus`: zero for every candidate (no one
##      submitted in C++), so trivially dropped.
##    - `q1_java`, `q2_java`, `q1_python`, `q2_python`: collinear with the
##      listed-language binaries in this 18-resume sample (fixest drops them).
##  Removed for endogeneity / interpretability:
##    - `code_opt_out`: students self-select into opting out based on their
##      own (unobserved) code quality, so the "validity" of this signal is
##      mechanically driven by the consent design rather than by anything the
##      evaluator could rationally use. Coefficient was never significant in
##      either §2.2 or §3, so dropping it loses no statistical content.
## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
hr_signals <- c("gpa_z",
                "num_proj", "num_exp", "num_lead", "num_awards",
                "work_1000", "work_1000_twice", "work_newstartup",
                "work_research", "work_teaching",
                "javascript", "c_base", "cplusplus", "csharp", "sql")

eng_only   <- c("test_case_z")

eng_signals <- c(hr_signals, eng_only)

fml_hr <- as.formula(paste0(
  "sc_overall_z ~ (", paste(hr_signals, collapse = " + "),
  ") * i(treat, ref = 'nonblind') | resume_index + responseid + r_do"
))
fml_eng <- as.formula(paste0(
  "sc_overall_z ~ (", paste(eng_signals, collapse = " + "),
  ") * i(treat, ref = 'nonblind') | resume_index + responseid + r_do"
))

m_hr  <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
  feols(fml_hr,  data = firm_long_dt[role == "HR"],
        vcov = ~ responseid + resume_id)
))
m_eng <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
  feols(fml_eng, data = firm_long_dt[role == "Eng"],
        vcov = ~ responseid + resume_id)
))

models_list <- list("HR" = m_hr, "Engineer" = m_eng)

## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
## Display labels and grouping for the forest plot + coef_map for the table.
## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
signal_meta <- data.table(
  raw     = c(hr_signals, eng_only),
  display = c("GPA (Z)",
              "\\# Projects", "\\# Experiences", "\\# Leadership", "\\# Awards",
              "Top-1000 Firm", "Top-1000 Firm (2x)", "New Startup",
              "Research Position", "Teaching Position",
              "JavaScript (listed)",
              "C (listed)", "C++ (listed)", "C\\# (listed)", "SQL (listed)",
              "Test Cases (Z)"),
  group   = c("Academic",
              rep("Resume Counts",     4),
              rep("Job Prestige",      5),
              rep("Languages Listed",  5),
              rep("Code Signal (Eng-only)", 1))
)
signal_meta[, group := factor(group, levels = c("Academic", "Resume Counts",
                                                "Job Prestige", "Languages Listed",
                                                "Code Signal (Eng-only)"))]

coef_map <- setNames(signal_meta$display, paste0(signal_meta$raw, ":treat::blind"))

## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
## FE / GoF scaffolding per project convention. Resume content (resume_index),
## display position (r_do), and respondent (responseid) FEs are all absorbed,
## which is the whole point of the spec. Respondent Characteristics and
## Q-version FE are dominated by Respondent FE, so listed `nox`.
## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
chk <- "\\checkmark"; nox <- "$\\times$"
fe_rows <- data.frame(
  term = c("Display Position FE",
           "Resume ID FE",
           "Resume - Coding Test Version FE",
           "Respondent ID FE",
           "Respondent Age (Cont) and Demographic FEs"),
  V1 = c(chk, chk, nox, chk, nox),
  V2 = c(chk, chk, nox, chk, nox),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
names(fe_rows) <- c("term", names(models_list))
## Inline est+se format -> 16 unique coef rows; FE rows (5) inserted at
## 17:21 (between the coef block and the GoF block, so Adj R^2 stays
## adjacent to Obs / R^2 in the rendered table).
attr(fe_rows, "position") <- 17:21

gof_map <- list(
  list("raw" = "nobs",          "clean" = "Obs.",       "fmt" = function(x) format(x, big.mark = ",")),
  list("raw" = "r.squared",     "clean" = "$R^2$",      "fmt" = function(x) sprintf("%.3f", x)),
  list("raw" = "adj.r.squared", "clean" = "Adj. $R^2$", "fmt" = function(x) sprintf("%.3f", x))
)

ms_tex <- modelsummary(
  models_list,
  estimate  = "{estimate}{stars} ({std.error})",
  statistic = NULL,
  output    = "kableExtra",
  coef_map  = coef_map,
  gof_map   = gof_map,
  add_rows  = fe_rows,
  stars     = c("*" = 0.10, "**" = 0.05, "***" = 0.01),
  escape    = FALSE,
  align     = "lcc"
) |> kableExtra::kable_styling(
       latex_options = c("HOLD_position"),
       font_size = 6, full_width = FALSE
     ) |> kableExtra::pack_rows("Academic",                  1,  1,  hline_after = FALSE) |>
        kableExtra::pack_rows("Resume Counts",             2,  5,  hline_after = FALSE) |>
        kableExtra::pack_rows("Job Prestige",              6, 10,  hline_after = FALSE) |>
        kableExtra::pack_rows("Languages Listed",         11, 15,  hline_after = FALSE) |>
        kableExtra::pack_rows("Code Signal (Eng-only)",   16, 16,  hline_after = FALSE) |>
        kableExtra::row_spec(16, extra_latex_after = "\\midrule") |>
        kableExtra::row_spec(21, extra_latex_after = "\\midrule")

## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
## Forest plot of signal x Blind coefficients, faceted by role and grouped.
## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
extract_signals <- function(m, role_label) {
  ct <- coeftable(m)
  blind_terms <- grep(":treat::blind$", rownames(ct), value = TRUE)
  if (length(blind_terms) == 0L) return(NULL)
  data.table(
    role     = role_label,
    raw      = sub(":treat::blind$", "", blind_terms),
    estimate = ct[blind_terms, "Estimate"],
    se       = ct[blind_terms, "Std. Error"],
    p_val    = ct[blind_terms, 4]
  )
}

dt_plot <- rbind(extract_signals(m_hr,  "HR Stage"),
                 extract_signals(m_eng, "Engineer Stage"))
dt_plot[, ci_low  := estimate - 1.96 * se]
dt_plot[, ci_high := estimate + 1.96 * se]

## Drop LaTeX escapes from display labels for the plot (gg can't parse \\#).
signal_meta_plot <- copy(signal_meta)
signal_meta_plot[, display_plot := gsub("\\\\#", "#", display)]
dt_plot <- dt_plot[signal_meta_plot, on = "raw", nomatch = NULL]
dt_plot[, role := factor(role, levels = c("HR Stage", "Engineer Stage"))]
dt_plot[, signal_factor := factor(display_plot,
                                   levels = rev(unique(signal_meta_plot$display_plot)))]
dt_plot[, sig_stars := fcase(p_val < 0.01, "***", p_val < 0.05, "**", p_val < 0.10, "*", default = "")]
dt_plot[, sig_color := fcase(p_val < 0.05, "Sig (p<0.05)", default = "n.s.")]

p_obj <- ggplot(dt_plot, aes(x = estimate, y = signal_factor, color = sig_color)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = ci_low, xmax = ci_high), size = 0.3, fatten = 2) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sig_stars, x = ci_high), hjust = -0.3, size = 2.4, color = "black") +
  facet_grid(group ~ role, scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Sig (p<0.05)" = "#C44E52", "n.s." = "gray40"),
                     name = NULL) +
  labs(x = "Coef. on Signal × Blind", y = NULL) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 6) +
  theme(
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.key.size    = unit(0.25, "cm"),
    legend.text        = element_text(size = 5),
    strip.text.x       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 6),
    strip.text.y       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 5, angle = 0),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 5),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 5),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(size = 6),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    plot.margin        = margin(2, 2, 2, 2, "pt")
  )

## Save plot, strip kableExtra's \begin{table}/\caption/\end{table} so the
## tabular fits inside a minipage, and lay out side-by-side.
fig_dir <- here("2_Reports", "figures")
if (!dir.exists(fig_dir)) dir.create(fig_dir, recursive = TRUE)
plot_file <- file.path(fig_dir, "8B_signal_substitution_sidebyside.pdf")
suppressMessages(suppressWarnings({
  ggsave(plot_file, plot = p_obj, width = 4, height = 4.2, device = "pdf")
}))

ms_str <- paste(as.character(ms_tex), collapse = "\n")
ms_str <- gsub("\\\\begin\\{table\\}[^\n]*\n?", "", ms_str)
ms_str <- gsub("\\\\caption\\{[^}]*\\}\n?", "",   ms_str)
ms_str <- gsub("\\\\end\\{table\\}\\s*", "",      ms_str)

cat("```{=latex}\n")
cat("\\noindent\\begin{minipage}[t]{0.52\\textwidth}\\centering\n")
cat("\\refstepcounter{table}\\textbf{Table~\\thetable: Signal Substitution}\\par\n")
cat("{\\small \\textit{Blinding: Change in Signal Weights on Evaluator-Assigned Overall Scores}}\\par\\smallskip\n")
cat(ms_str, "\n", sep = "")
cat("\\end{minipage}\\hfill\n")
cat("\\begin{minipage}[t]{0.46\\textwidth}\\centering\n")
cat("\\refstepcounter{figure}\\textbf{Figure~\\thefigure: Signal Substitution}\\par\n")
cat("{\\small \\textit{Blinding: Change in Signal Weights on Evaluator-Assigned Overall Scores}}\\par\\smallskip\n")
cat("\\includegraphics[width=\\textwidth]{", plot_file, "}\n", sep = "")
cat("\\end{minipage}\n")
cat("```\n")

rm(hr_signals, eng_only, eng_signals, fml_hr, fml_eng, m_hr, m_eng, models_list,
   signal_meta, signal_meta_plot, coef_map, fe_rows, chk, nox, gof_map, ms_tex,
   p_obj, dt_plot, extract_signals, plot_file, fig_dir, ms_str)
```

\clearpage
## Re-weighing of Internal Rubric -- Coding / Fit / Stay Dimension Aggregation

\textbf{Question:} Do the weights evaluators place on the three sub-dimension ratings (Coding Skill, Fit, Stay) shift under blinding?

\textbf{Specification:}
\vspace{-0.7em}
$$
\begin{aligned}
\text{Overall\_Evaluation\_Score~(Z)}_{ij} \;\sim\;& \sum_{d \in \{\text{Coding\_Skill},\,\text{Fit},\,\text{Stay}\}} \big(\beta_d + \boldsymbol{\gamma_d}\,\mathbbm{1}[\text{Blind}]_j\big)\,\text{Score}_d^i \\
& \;\Big|\; \text{Resume\_ID~FE}_i + \text{Respondent\_ID~FE}_j + \text{Display\_Order~FE}_{ij} \\[2pt]
& \text{Two-way clustered SEs on (responseid, resume\_id).}
\end{aligned}
$$
\vspace{-1em}

```{r internal_rubric_shift, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

if (!"resume_id" %in% names(firm_long_dt)) {
  firm_long_dt[, resume_id := paste(resume_ver, resume_index, sep = "_")]
}

m_hr <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
  feols(sc_overall_z ~ (sc_coding_z + sc_fit_z + sc_stay_z) * i(treat, ref = "nonblind")
        | responseid + resume_index + r_do,
        data = firm_long_dt[role == "HR"],
        vcov = ~ responseid + resume_id)
))
m_eng <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
  feols(sc_overall_z ~ (sc_coding_z + sc_fit_z + sc_stay_z) * i(treat, ref = "nonblind")
        | responseid + resume_index + r_do,
        data = firm_long_dt[role == "Eng"],
        vcov = ~ responseid + resume_id)
))

models_list <- list("HR" = m_hr, "Engineer" = m_eng)

coef_map <- c(
  "sc_coding_z"              = "Coding (Z)",
  "sc_fit_z"                 = "Fit (Z)",
  "sc_stay_z"                = "Stay (Z)",
  "sc_coding_z:treat::blind" = "Coding $\\times$ Blind",
  "sc_fit_z:treat::blind"    = "Fit $\\times$ Blind",
  "sc_stay_z:treat::blind"   = "Stay $\\times$ Blind"
)

chk <- "\\checkmark"; nox <- "$\\times$"
fe_rows <- data.frame(
  term = c("Display Position FE",
           "Resume ID FE",
           "Resume - Coding Test Version FE",
           "Respondent ID FE",
           "Respondent Age (Cont) and Demographic FEs"),
  V1 = c(chk, chk, nox, chk, nox),
  V2 = c(chk, chk, nox, chk, nox),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
names(fe_rows) <- c("term", names(models_list))
## Inline est+se -> 6 unique coef rows; FE rows (5) at 7:11.
attr(fe_rows, "position") <- 7:11

gof_map <- list(
  list("raw" = "nobs",          "clean" = "Obs.",       "fmt" = function(x) format(x, big.mark = ",")),
  list("raw" = "r.squared",     "clean" = "$R^2$",      "fmt" = function(x) sprintf("%.3f", x)),
  list("raw" = "adj.r.squared", "clean" = "Adj. $R^2$", "fmt" = function(x) sprintf("%.3f", x))
)

ms_tex <- modelsummary(
  models_list,
  estimate  = "{estimate}{stars} ({std.error})",
  statistic = NULL,
  output    = "kableExtra",
  coef_map  = coef_map,
  gof_map   = gof_map,
  add_rows  = fe_rows,
  stars     = c("*" = 0.10, "**" = 0.05, "***" = 0.01),
  escape    = FALSE,
  align     = "lcc",
  title     = "Internal Rubric Shift"
) |> kableExtra::kable_styling(
       latex_options = c("HOLD_position"),
       font_size = 8, full_width = FALSE
     ) |> kableExtra::pack_rows("Sub-dimension level (nonblind baseline)", 1, 3, hline_after = FALSE) |>
        kableExtra::pack_rows("Re-weighting under Blind Evaluation", 4, 6, hline_after = FALSE) |>
        kableExtra::row_spec(6,  extra_latex_after = "\\midrule") |>
        kableExtra::row_spec(11, extra_latex_after = "\\midrule")
print(ms_tex)

rm(m_hr, m_eng, models_list, coef_map, fe_rows, chk, nox, gof_map, ms_tex)
```

\clearpage
# Signal Validity: Are Visible Signals Predictive of True Coding Ability?

\textit{(NEED TO RE-RUN WITH ALL 69 STUDENT RESUMES)}

\textbf{Why this matters.} §2.2 shows that under blinding, evaluators reweight their attention onto visible resume signals --- but a positive weight-shift only counts as rational signal substitution if those signals \emph{actually predict} true productivity. If the signals blinding pushes evaluators toward have zero validity, the §2.2 effect is just \emph{credentialism} (the flight-to-credentials hypothesis). This section provides the validity benchmark.


\textbf{Question:} Among the visible resume signals evaluators rely on, which actually predict true productivity?

\textbf{Specification:}
\vspace{-0.7em}
$$
\begin{aligned}
\text{True\_Productivity\_Score~(Z)}_{i} \;\sim\;& \alpha + \boldsymbol{\gamma_s}\,\text{Signal}_s^i + \varepsilon_i,\quad i = 1, \dots, 18 \\[2pt]
& \text{One univariate OLS per signal at the resume level; HC1 robust SE.}
\end{aligned}
$$
\vspace{-1em}
```{r signal_validity_pooled, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, fig.width=11, fig.height=7}

if (!"resume_id" %in% names(firm_long_dt)) {
  firm_long_dt[, resume_id := paste(resume_ver, resume_index, sep = "_")]
}
## true_coding_z is canonically built in §6 (Productivity of Hired); this
## section now runs before that, so build it defensively here if missing.
if (!"true_coding_z" %in% names(firm_long_dt)) {
  firm_long_dt[, true_coding_z := (overall_score - mean(overall_score, na.rm = TRUE)) /
                                    sd(overall_score, na.rm = TRUE)]
}

## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
## Same visible-signal sets as §2.2. python / java / q1+q2 language indicators
## omitted (constants or collinear, see §2.2 caption); code_opt_out omitted as
## endogenous (students self-select into opting out based on their own code
## quality, so its 'validity' is mechanically driven by the consent design).
## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
hr_signals <- c("gpa_z",
                "num_proj", "num_exp", "num_lead", "num_awards",
                "work_1000", "work_1000_twice", "work_newstartup",
                "work_research", "work_teaching",
                "javascript", "c_base", "cplusplus", "csharp", "sql")

eng_only   <- c("test_case_z")
all_signals <- c(hr_signals, eng_only)

signal_meta <- data.table(
  raw     = all_signals,
  display = c("GPA (Z)",
              "\\# Projects", "\\# Experiences", "\\# Leadership", "\\# Awards",
              "Top-1000 Firm", "Top-1000 Firm (2x)", "New Startup",
              "Research Position", "Teaching Position",
              "JavaScript (listed)",
              "C (listed)", "C++ (listed)", "C\\# (listed)", "SQL (listed)",
              "Test Cases (Z)"),
  group   = c("Academic",
              rep("Resume Counts",     4),
              rep("Job Prestige",      5),
              rep("Languages Listed",  5),
              rep("Code Signal (Eng-only)", 1))
)

## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
## Resume-level data (one row per unique resume_index). Univariate validity
## is identified at the cross-resume level (N = 18); 5,238 candidate-evaluator
## panel rows are just 291 replications of these 18 cross-resume points.
## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
resume_dt <- unique(firm_long_dt[, c("resume_index", "true_coding_z", all_signals),
                                 with = FALSE])

## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
## Univariate validity regressions: one OLS per signal. HC1 robust SE.
## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
univ_results <- rbindlist(lapply(all_signals, function(s) {
  fml <- as.formula(paste0("true_coding_z ~ ", s))
  m <- tryCatch(feols(fml, data = resume_dt, vcov = "hetero"),
                error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(m) || !s %in% rownames(coeftable(m)))
    return(data.table(raw = s, val_est = NA_real_, val_se = NA_real_,
                      val_p = NA_real_, val_r2 = NA_real_, val_n = NA_integer_))
  ct <- coeftable(m)
  data.table(raw = s,
             val_est = ct[s, "Estimate"],
             val_se  = ct[s, "Std. Error"],
             val_p   = ct[s, 4],
             val_r2  = fitstat(m, "r2",   verbose = FALSE)$r2,
             val_n   = nobs(m))
}))
univ_results <- univ_results[signal_meta, on = "raw"]
univ_results[, group := factor(group, levels = c("Academic", "Resume Counts",
                                                  "Job Prestige", "Languages Listed",
                                                  "Code Signal (Eng-only)"))]

## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
## §5 weight-shift regressions, re-fit here so we can extract signal x Blind
## coefficients for the §5 vs §6 scatter (the §5 chunk rm()'d its objects).
## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
fml_wt_hr <- as.formula(paste0(
  "sc_overall_z ~ (", paste(hr_signals, collapse = " + "),
  ") * i(treat, ref = 'nonblind') | resume_index + responseid + r_do"))
fml_wt_eng <- as.formula(paste0(
  "sc_overall_z ~ (", paste(c(hr_signals, eng_only), collapse = " + "),
  ") * i(treat, ref = 'nonblind') | resume_index + responseid + r_do"))

m_wt_hr  <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
  feols(fml_wt_hr,  data = firm_long_dt[role == "HR"],
        vcov = ~ responseid + resume_id)))
m_wt_eng <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
  feols(fml_wt_eng, data = firm_long_dt[role == "Eng"],
        vcov = ~ responseid + resume_id)))

extract_weight <- function(m, role_label) {
  ct <- coeftable(m)
  blind_terms <- grep(":treat::blind$", rownames(ct), value = TRUE)
  if (length(blind_terms) == 0L) return(NULL)
  data.table(
    role = role_label,
    raw  = sub(":treat::blind$", "", blind_terms),
    wt_est = ct[blind_terms, "Estimate"],
    wt_se  = ct[blind_terms, "Std. Error"]
  )
}
dt_wt <- rbind(extract_weight(m_wt_hr,  "HR Stage"),
               extract_weight(m_wt_eng, "Engineer Stage"))

## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
## Univariate validity table. Custom kable (single-column estimate (SE) +
## R^2 + N), pack_rows by signal group.
## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
fmt_cell <- function(est, se, p) {
  star <- fcase(p < 0.01, "***", p < 0.05, "**", p < 0.10, "*", default = "")
  sprintf("%.3f%s (%.3f)", est, star, se)
}
tbl <- data.table(
  Signal      = univ_results$display,
  Estimate    = mapply(fmt_cell, univ_results$val_est, univ_results$val_se, univ_results$val_p),
  `$R^2$`     = sprintf("%.3f", univ_results$val_r2),
  `Obs.`      = format(univ_results$val_n, big.mark = ",")
)
print(
  kable(tbl, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE, escape = FALSE,
        align = c("l", "c", "c", "c"),
        caption = "Signal Validity --- univariate cross-resume slope of True Productivity Score (Z) on each visible signal.") %>%
    kable_styling(latex_options = c("HOLD_position"),
                  font_size = 8, full_width = FALSE) %>%
    pack_rows("Academic",                1,  1,  hline_after = FALSE) %>%
    pack_rows("Resume Counts",           2,  5,  hline_after = FALSE) %>%
    pack_rows("Job Prestige",            6, 10,  hline_after = FALSE) %>%
    pack_rows("Languages Listed",       11, 15,  hline_after = FALSE) %>%
    pack_rows("Code Signal (Eng-only)", 16, 16,  hline_after = FALSE)
)

## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
## §5 vs §6 scatter. x-axis = §5 multivariate signal x Blind coef (per role);
## y-axis = §6 univariate validity coef (same value for both roles since
## truth doesn't depend on the evaluator). Quadrants tell the storyline.
## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
dt_scatter <- merge(dt_wt,
                    univ_results[, .(raw, val_est, val_se,
                                      display_plot = gsub("\\\\#", "#", display),
                                      group)],
                    by = "raw")
dt_scatter[, role := factor(role, levels = c("HR Stage", "Engineer Stage"))]

p_scatter <- ggplot(dt_scatter, aes(x = wt_est, y = val_est, color = group)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = wt_est  - 1.96 * wt_se,  xmax = wt_est  + 1.96 * wt_se),
                 height = 0, alpha = 0.4, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin  = val_est - 1.96 * val_se, ymax = val_est + 1.96 * val_se),
                width = 0,  alpha = 0.4, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_point(size = 2.4) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(aes(label = display_plot),
                            size = 2.7, max.overlaps = 25, force = 2,
                            box.padding = 0.4, segment.alpha = 0.5,
                            color = "gray20") +
  facet_wrap(~ role, ncol = 2, scales = "fixed") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Academic"            = "#9E5BB7",
                                "Resume Counts"       = "#4C72B0",
                                "Job Prestige"        = "#C44E52",
                                "Languages Listed"    = "#55A868",
                                "Code Signal (Eng-only)" = "#DD8452"),
                     name = NULL) +
  labs(
    subtitle = "Signal weight shift under blinding vs. signal validity for true productivity",
    x = "Change in evaluator's weight on this signal under blinding  --  blinding makes evaluators weigh the signal MORE (right) / LESS (left)",
    y = "How well this signal predicts true productivity  --  predictive (up) / not predictive (near 0)",
    caption = "Top-right quadrant: rational re-weighting (blinding shifts evaluators toward genuinely predictive signals). Top-left: missed opportunity (signal is predictive but evaluators weigh it less under blinding). Bottom-right: flight to credentials (blinding shifts toward signals with no validity). Bottom-left: rational drop. Vertical bars: 95% CI on the weight-shift coefficient; horizontal bars: 95% CI on the validity slope."
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray30"),
    strip.text      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    plot.caption    = element_text(size = 8, hjust = 0)
  )
print(p_scatter)

rm(hr_signals, eng_only, all_signals, signal_meta, resume_dt, univ_results,
   fml_wt_hr, fml_wt_eng, m_wt_hr, m_wt_eng, extract_weight,
   dt_wt, dt_scatter, fmt_cell, tbl, p_scatter)
```





\clearpage
# Individual Response to Institutional Policy (Institutional Crowding Out or Compensatory Bias)

\textit{Takeaway / Summary.}
\begin{itemize}\setlength\itemsep{2pt}\setlength\parskip{0pt}\setlength\topsep{0pt}\setlength\partopsep{0pt}
  \item \textbf{The Puzzle of Individual Ideology:} The two-way relationship between an evaluator's personal DEI priority and their evaluation scores is surprisingly \emph{muted and statistically noisy}. At first glance, personal ideology appears to have little impact on how blind hiring reshuffles outcomes.
  \begin{itemize}\setlength\itemsep{2pt}\setlength\parskip{0pt}\setlength\topsep{0pt}\setlength\partopsep{0pt}
    \item \textbf{Compensatory Bias} (Firms with NO Diversity Mandate, see \textcolor{red}{red lines}): When a firm lacks an explicit objective policy targeting URMs, highly pro-DEI HR professionals engage in \emph{compensatory bias} --- filling the perceived institutional void by informally inflating minority candidates' scores in the baseline.
    \item \textbf{Institutional Crowding-Out} (Firms WITH a Diversity Mandate, see \textcolor{blue}{blue lines}): Conversely, when the firm explicitly mandates URM targeting, this formal policy \emph{crowds out} the evaluator's informal efforts; pro-DEI HR professionals \emph{mute} their premium to under-represented minorities' scores.
  \end{itemize}
  \item (Note the \emph{divergence between HR and Engineer results}, particularly in the triple-interaction columns.)
\end{itemize}
\vspace{0.4em}

\textit{Note on Key Index Creation.}
\begin{itemize}\setlength\itemsep{2pt}\setlength\parskip{0pt}\setlength\topsep{0pt}\setlength\partopsep{0pt}
  \item \textit{Individual DEI Index (Z)}: Evaluator's personal DEI orientation, condensed into a single z-scored score. (Low = Anti-DEI, High = Pro-DEI)
  \begin{itemize}\setlength\itemsep{1pt}\setlength\parskip{0pt}\setlength\topsep{0pt}
    \item Mathematical expression: $\text{Index} = z\big(\tfrac{1}{5}\sum_{j=1}^{5} z(x_j)\big)$, z-scored within (\texttt{role}, \texttt{treat}) cells.
    \item \texttt{pro\_dei\_race} --- stated agreement that race DEI is important in hiring (Likert).
    \item \texttt{pro\_dei\_gender} --- stated agreement that gender DEI is important in hiring (Likert).
    \item \texttt{pro\_diverse} --- stated importance of working at a company with a diverse workforce (Likert).
    \item \texttt{pro\_hiring\_dei} --- stated belief that DEI initiatives help firms hire better candidates (Likert).
    \item \texttt{div\_priority} --- revealed-priority rank of ``Diversity and Representation'' in the seven-goals question.
  \end{itemize}
  \item \textit{Firm Targets Fem/His (0/1)}: Indicator for firm policy --- whether the evaluator's firm formally targets Female or Hispanic candidates in hiring.
  \begin{itemize}\setlength\itemsep{1pt}\setlength\parskip{0pt}\setlength\topsep{0pt}
    \item Mathematical expression: $\text{Idx} = \mathbb{1}\{\texttt{firm\_targets\_female} = 1 \;\lor\; \texttt{firm\_targets\_hispanic} = 1\}$.
    \item \texttt{firm\_targets\_female} --- 1 if option 6 (``Female'') was selected on \texttt{s5\_policy\_diversity} (multi-select).
    \item \texttt{firm\_targets\_hispanic} --- 1 if option 3 (``Hispanic or Latino'') was selected on the same multi-select.
  \end{itemize}
  \item \textit{Firm DEI Attn Index (Z)}: Respondent's perception of how much attention their firm currently pays to race and gender DEI in hiring, condensed into a single z-scored score.
  \begin{itemize}\setlength\itemsep{1pt}\setlength\parskip{0pt}\setlength\topsep{0pt}
    \item Mathematical expression: $\text{Index} = z\big(\tfrac{1}{2}\big(z(\texttt{race\_attn}) + z(\texttt{gender\_attn})\big)\big)$, z-scored within (\texttt{role}, \texttt{treat}) cells.
    \item \texttt{s5\_view\_dei\_race\_f} --- Likert: respondent's perception of firm's current attention to race DEI in hiring.
    \item \texttt{s5\_view\_dei\_gender\_f} --- Likert: respondent's perception of firm's current attention to gender DEI in hiring.
  \end{itemize}
\end{itemize}
\vspace{-0.6em}

\clearpage
## By Six Demographics

```{r bh_dei_combined_rg, echo = FALSE, results = 'asis', warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
## Replicates the §4.5 / §4.6 combined structure but using the full
## per-race-gender breakdown (six demographics, as in §4.1). Single combined
## table with three specs: simple DEI het | triple Firm Targets URM | triple
## Firm DEI Attn. DEI proxy: Index A only. Coefficients flipped post-fit so
## positive => effect of blinding raises the score for that group.
## Estimate and standard error are placed on the same line so all 36 coef
## rows + FE rows + N row fit on a single landscape page.

dt_hr  <- firm_long_dt[role == "HR"]
dt_eng <- firm_long_dt[role == "Eng"]

dt_hr [, firm_targets_fh := as.integer(firm_targets_female == 1L | firm_targets_hispanic == 1L)]
